item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The images that were used in this article are non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) as the first image could not be sourced and the second image uses a component which is licensed under an NC "non-commercial" and ND "non-derivative" license (seen here). Thus, the images have been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So the ghost car causes accidents by playing chicken with people, so that they swerve and hit something else… Now I'm curious about what happens if you don't swerve. Can you actually hit the Thunderbird, or is it a mirage? Might confronting it produce an entirely different result?

+1 for the thought of the Foundation being inconvenienced by trying to get anything done in a high-traffic area, just like everyone else.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A tad cliche but the runic induced ghost car from an extremist protest group is quite a nice spin on it. Why safe though? The runes aren't understood at all and this is more than enough to kill someone without proper management, seems a little more Euclid to me.

I like it though, you have my vote!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Meh. Ghost car. No hook for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just a minor nitpick, but I see it on this site a lot and it really bugs me: there is no logical justification for using a percentage - especially when it's not a round number like 50% - to denote a proportion of a number less than 100. The blackbox for the number of incidents is 2 digits long (actually, why does the number of incidents need to be blackboxed anyway?), so if you must use a percentage it should be 90% rather than 93%.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wait, why did I have this downvoted? This is great.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this one; it's solid, and (apparently post-rewrite) it's got a good little story going. +1.

One issue remains for me, though. If the car appears like clockwork every year at the same time, why on Earth is the Foundation permitting civilian traffic through the south tunnel at that time?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this one. The one thing I'll get nitpicky about is with that image - police dashboard cameras didn't come into use until the late 1980s. I don't really think it takes anything away from the article, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ooooooooh, you can't kill the ghoooooost meeeeeeme

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's a solid addition and I'd say a definite improvement.
Addendum 2 provides a good reason for the crate to be the main item and more clearly defines a reason for that Ford to be the ghost car.
And yet, I still don't like the article. I think at this point it might be echoes of my first poor reaction that I just can't shake, but there it is. Since I can't point to anything wrong anymore, I'll neutral vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: And we have our connection. I'll be honest, it still doesn't do a whole lot for me, but you have improved it considerably. Removing my downvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Dang, I remembered the story and considered the possibility, but discounted it when I counted one too few blackboxes for Caldecott. That said, decent SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yup!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
People above asked for more explanation. (The original draft did not include Addendum 2.)

Thanks for reading, and glad you mostly enjoyed it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hey, take a look at Addendum-2 and let me know if that changes your overall opinion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yesssss it issss.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
sigh

Is this going to become a "thing" now? Anything that could remotely be considered a ghost is going to have stupid "ghost sign" comments?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Now that sucks. That's my birthday. Somehow that makes me sad.

I'll upvote anyway. Though Adendum 2 feels unneeded for me, why do we need an explanation or connection? Why can't freaky shit just be there, somewhat tacked together, if it works?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fixed. Thanks for the notes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well done. +1

You managed to add in just the right amount of weird, without going too weird. That's a hard thing to do, and you've managed it. You should still fix up those blacked out dates, but that's nitpicking.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh god, that's a matchup I really want to see

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Cool. I liked your suggestions and made those fixes.

Also added Addendum-2. Let me know if it does anything for you. Thanks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Added addendum-2. Let me know if that does anything for you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Added Addendum-2. Let me know if that improves the article for you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The two sections of this article don't really seem connected in the least. As someone else said, it may as well just be coincidence at this point. I'm not seeing anything that suggests any kind of relation between them apart from geography, but then I'm also pretty bad at finding those.

That said, this is also way short. Like, it just sort of stops, leaving me with a feeling of, "That's it?" No hook or anything, though that second picture is pretty great.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A couple things I think need fixing:

You don't need to expunge the dates/times. It adds nothing and makes the article harder to read.

The mysterious container: You don't really tie it in to the effects. Use it or lose it…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If it did something crazy, or even just odd (actually, odd is better than crazy), I would probably upvote. I'm just spitballing here, but perhaps the Firebird could change with the era. It started in the 50's, but maybe in the 60's, it becomes a VW Bug, or something.

There's a ton of options you can take here to spice up the article, and all of them are pretty good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: These are good suggestions. I'll think on it and make some edits today.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Sorry you didn't like the concept, and thanks for the notes.

My last 2 articles (1 success, 1 failure) have been pretty long & detailed. My goal here was to write something simple.

If I added some details about what happens when the car hits you (or passes through you), would that added complexity do anything for you? And if so, any thoughts/suggestions for that effect?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm going to have to 2nd this. As this stands right now, the box seems almost like an afterthought, and doesn't really affect this article at all. The SCP could just as easily be the car itself. In that case, this is just too similar to all those creepypastas about "If you drive your car along this highway at night, you will see a car…" etc. It is cool that you based it off an actual event, but that doesn't mean much to people who don't know about it.
Perhaps expanding more about the nature of the box, repeated attempts to break into it, and hinting at what it has to do with the Thunderbird, could make this article something better, but for now, downvoting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's basically a ghost car that hates people. I'm sorry, it's just not a very original or interesting concept for me. Downvoted.

However, I do have a few other notes:

discovered buried in a hillside in ██████ █████ County, California, in 19██.

The date doesn't need to be blackboxed, in my opinion. Same goes for any date in the page. If it's not confidential information, why withhold it?

It appears each year on █████ █ at approximately ██:██ hours, and it has appeared on other occasions after SCP-1343 has been disturbed.

That would seem to be a very, very important part of the description. It's not like you redacted something in the Containment Procedures (kudos on that), but this still doesn't need to be blackboxed.

Overall, the execution is pretty good. For me, it's the concept that fails.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The item is a big box. Someone tried to open it once, and a bad traffic accident happened nearby. Now there is a ghost car from that accident…
Nope, I don't like it.

First, you need more on the container. One accident is a coincidence; if there is a disaster every time they try to open it, move it, x-ray it, what have you, then you've got something anomalous.
Second, there is no connection between the box and the ghost car. Why that Ford and not the other flaming wrecks? There doesn't need to be a conclusive forensic trail, but we poor readers need at least a hint of a connection, even if it's just fridge logic.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Nice. I'm originally from Berkeley.

Went to college on the East Coast, and then lived there for a few years after. Now back in Berkeley for the summer.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yep, I'm from there, and I was thinking of that incident as I was reading it, as a matter of fact. I went to college in the immediate vicinity, and I used to tease girlfriends as we drove through the tunnel. "Boy, sure hope there aren't any fuel tankers out tonight", etc.

Nicely done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah it is. You from the bay?

The accident is based on a real accident: http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Caldecott_Tunnel_fire

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's the ghost car. -1.

Well, okay. I like the rewrite a lot better. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You know why the ghost car is on fire?

BECAUSE IT FAILED TO YIELD.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You mean the ghoooooosssttttt Caaaaaaaaaaar!

Destineeedddd to do baaattllle with the ghoooooossst siiiiiigggnn!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's the ghost car. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is that the Caldecott Tunnel?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Short, weird and yet enjoyable. Upvoted.
